steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the linear scale factor
steps.content: Each side of parallelogram WXYZ is 3 times the corresponding side of parallelogram JKLM. The linear scale factor is therefore $k = 3$.

steps.title: Apply area scaling for similar figures
steps.content:
For similar figures, areas scale by the square of the linear scale factor. With $k = 3$, the area scales by $k^2 = 3^2 = 9$.
$$\text{Area of WXYZ} = (\text{Area of JKLM}) \times 9 = 4 \times 9 = 36 \text{ cm}^2$$
That confirms C is correct.
